page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 virtual CPUs, representing 24 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, delivering high-speed local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. Networking capabilities are defined by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps, optimized for both general network traffic and Elastic Block Store performance. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core performance and image processing. Cryptographic operations are also highly efficient, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Other metrics, including single-core processing, memory bandwidth, and LLM inference speeds, fall within the average range for its hardware class.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6idn.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density by combining high-speed local NVMe storage, substantial memory, and high network throughput within a single general-purpose profile. This eliminates the need for separate dedicated storage instances for many data-intensive tasks. The hardware configuration is suited for workloads such as relational databases, distributed data stores, containerized applications, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core performance, particularly in image processing and cryptographic encryption tests, where it ranks in the top 25%. Other metrics, such as memory bandwidth and single-core execution, align with average tier expectations. This resource profile provides qualitative cost efficiency by bundling high-speed local storage and high network throughput with balanced compute resources. The instance is suited for database hosting, data processing, and enterprise application workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps bandwidth, suited for databases and multi-core workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 75 Gbps baseline networking.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
